svg.aria-label: E-Commerce MCP Solution Accelerator — Azure reference architecture
svg.title: E-Commerce MCP Solution Accelerator — Azure reference architecture (production path)
svg.desc: A Python client calls a Microsoft Foundry agent over the Responses API. The agent uses an MCPTool to call a Playwright MCP server hosted on Azure Container Apps. The container image comes from the public Microsoft Container Registry (mcr.microsoft.com/playwright). Headless Chromium browses target e-commerce sites. Authentication uses Microsoft Entra ID via DefaultAzureCredential / managed identity. Observability is provided by the Microsoft Foundry portal. Screenshot persistence in Azure Blob Storage is optional.
svg.text: E-Commerce MCP Solution Accelerator — Azure architecture (production path)
Foundry agent uses an MCPTool to call a Playwright MCP server hosted on Azure Container Apps. Image is pulled from the public Microsoft Container Registry.

g.text: External HTTPS ingress · 1 vCPU / 2 GiB · auto-scale 1–3
Optional; not required by the accelerator
Client acquires a token from Microsoft Entra ID via DefaultAzureCredential / managed identity.
Client calls the Microsoft Foundry Responses API for the registered agent (name + version) over HTTPS.
The agent executes its MCPTool(server_url=…) and calls the Playwright MCP server in Azure Container Apps over HTTPS — server-side, no client proxy.
Container App pulls the public Playwright image from mcr.microsoft.com/playwright (no customer Container Registry required).
Headless Chromium inside the container browses the target e-commerce sites (Tier 1 price, Tier 2 geo, Tier 3 device emulation).
Optional: container persists screenshots to Azure Blob Storage. Foundry portal natively shows agent runs, traces, tokens & cost.
• Infra defined in production/infra/main.bicep; deployed by infra/deploy.sh.
• Identity: Foundry agent uses a system-assigned managed identity for ACA invocation.
• Observability: Microsoft Foundry portal (agent runs, token usage, traces) — no Azure Monitor needed.
• Networking: external HTTPS ingress; private VNet + Foundry private MCP networking is optional.
• Solid blue = primary flow. Dotted = identity/auth. Loose dash = optional path.
Logos: Microsoft Foundry and Playwright marks are the official logos. Azure service icons (Entra ID,
Container Apps, Container Apps Environment, Managed Identity, Blob Storage, Container Registries)
are from the official Microsoft Azure Public Service Icons set (V23).
